metadata.name: Division
metadata.value: Advertising

metadata.name: Employment Type
metadata.value: Full-time

metadata.name: Publish - Description (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
Are you ready to dive into the fast-paced world of digital advertising? We are looking for a motivated, detail-oriented Ad Monetization Coordinator to join our team at the cutting edge of in-game technology.
You will collaborate with industry experts to manage, analyze, and optimize global ad campaigns. If you enjoy turning complex data into meaningful insights and thrive in a role that combines technical execution with strategic communication, this is your opportunity to grow.

metadata.name: Publish - Responsibilities (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- Execute end-to-end setup, testing, and delivery of display, video, and native campaigns via ad servers.
- Continuously analyze performance trends and optimize partner campaigns to maximize revenue.
- Perform daily monitoring to identify and resolve technical discrepancies, ad-serving issues, and inappropriate ad content.
- Generate performance reports and translate metrics into actionable insights.
- Monitor revenue health and proactively flag performance fluctuations.
- Create and maintain internal documentation, contributing to the evolution of best practices.

metadata.name: Publish - Required Skills (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- Strong interpersonal skills and ability to work effectively within a team.
- Highly organized with excellent time management and a strong work ethic.
- Analytical mindset with strong data interpretation skills.
- Excellent communication skills, with fluency in English (written and spoken).

metadata.name: Publish - Required Experience (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- Currently pursuing or completed a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Marketing, Business, Communications, or a related field.
- Strong analytical abilities and high attention to detail.
- Proficiency in Microsoft Excel or Google Sheets.
- Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
- Ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
- Strong interest in technology.
- Basic familiarity with the digital advertising ecosystem (DSPs, SSPs, ad servers) is a plus.

metadata.name: Pubish - What Do We Offer
metadata.value:
- Employment relationship with Ekipa2 d.o.o. for an indefinite period of time, with a six-month probationary period.
- Diverse work with exciting daily challenges, working as part of a team in a fun, relaxed environment.
- Participation in attractive projects.
- Development opportunities through training, seminars, and conferences.
- Choice of sport, fitness, educational, and entertainment activities outside of work.
- Healthy meals in the office.
- Flexible working hours.
- 24 days of paid vacation.
- Participation in a discretionary bonus plan.
- Christmas bonus (subject to internal policies).

metadata.name: Job category (area)
metadata.value: Sales & Business Development

metadata.name: Recuiter to display
metadata.value: Neja Rozman

metadata.name: Division
metadata.value: Advertising

metadata.name: Employment Type
metadata.value: Full-time

metadata.name: Publish - Description (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
Excited about turning data into impact? Advertising is one of the fastest moving parts of our business, and we’re seeking a sharp mind to help push it further.
Join our Ad Monetization team and turn data into revenue—owning experiments, insights, and strategies that shape the future of our ad business. As an Ad Monetization Expert, you will work across teams to optimize systems, design A/B tests, and translate data into decisions that drive growth.
Your mission is to maximize revenue by combining analytical expertise, technical understanding, and strong business acumen. If that’s a mission you want to accept, then take this opportunity to make your mark.

metadata.name: Publish - Responsibilities (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- Perform daily performance monitoring and analyses to support decision-making, including gathering requirements, preparing datasets, interpreting results, and communicating insights.
- Apply a proactive 'get-it-done' mindset to daily operations to maintain consistent performance and operational stability.
- Collaborate on AB testing, including planning, creating test setup, monitoring performance during the tests, and preparing post-test reports and recommendations to support evidence-based decisions.
- Design, develop, and maintain monitoring tools, dashboards, and reports to track key performance indicators and business metrics.
- Collaborate with different teams to align outcomes and process improvements with strategic business goals.
- Coordinate and support cross-functional projects, ensuring alignment between sales, tech, and analytical teams.
- Actively contribute to the development and refinement of business strategies aimed at revenue growth and improved operational performance.
- Challenge existing workflows and procedures with a continuous improvement mindset, using data to support change initiatives.
- Stay up to date with internal tools, systems, and data sources to remain effective in analysis, monitoring, and coordination efforts.

metadata.name: Publish - Required Skills (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- An analytical & technical mindset with a "hands-on" approach and good attention to detail.
- The ability to work with large datasets, interpret trends, and translate complex data into simple, actionable insights.
- A pragmatic mindset with good business sense.
- Ability to work in a fast-paced environment - comfortable working in a dynamic environment with shifting priorities.
- Positive, make-it-happen, lead-by-example attitude.
- Ability to work with different teams (Analytics team, BE, Adtech, QA). 
- Strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to work effectively as part of a team.
- Strong communication skills, with fluency in the English language (both written and spoken).

metadata.name: Publish - Required Experience (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- 3+ years of experience in roles with a strong focus on data-driven operations, business support, or performance monitoring.
- Experience working with cross-functional teams, acting as a bridge between technical and business departments.
- Experience with coordinating/ in managing projects across multiple departments.
- Hands-on experience with data analysis, reporting, and performance monitoring.
- Proficiency with Excel and BI tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI) to support day-to-day decision-making.
- Experience with SQL is a plus.
- Knowledge of the advertising industry is a plus.

metadata.name: Pubish - What Do We Offer
metadata.value:
- Employment relationship with Ekipa2 d.o.o. for an indefinite period of time, with a six-month probationary period.
- Diverse work with exciting daily challenges, working as part of a team in a fun, relaxed environment.
- Participation in attractive projects.
- Development opportunities through training, seminars, and conferences.
- Choice of sport, fitness, educational, and entertainment activities outside of work.
- Healthy meals in the office.
- Flexible working hours.
- 24 days of paid vacation.
- Participation in a discretionary bonus plan.
- Christmas bonus (subject to internal policies).

metadata.name: Publish - Description (Recruiter)
metadata.value: We’re looking for a Chief Product Officer (CPO) to lead the next era of Outfit7’s growth. You’ll connect creativity with strategy, transforming ideas into world-class products that delight players and define what’s next in mobile entertainment.

metadata.name: Publish - Responsibilities (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- Define and lead the company’s products vision, strategy and goals, ensuring alignment with long-term business objectives.
- Oversee all games in Talking Tom and Friends product line, and other company games and entertainment directions, and roadmaps, managing cross-functional collaboration across design, development, marketing, data and operations.
- Supervise company-wide priorities, budgets and delivery processes, turning the vision into achievable plans.
- Lead and mentor senior managers and key roles, supporting their growth and empowering high-performing teams.
- Recognise and initiate strategic opportunities that move the company forward.
- Make and stand behind the most difficult decisions, communicating the “why” with clarity and confidence.
- Inspire others to share and live the company vision — keeping teams motivated, aligned, and connected.
- Anticipate risks and scenarios, lead through uncertainty with calm and clarity.
- Ensure that every product we deliver reflects the quality, creativity, and spirit that define Outfit7.

metadata.name: Publish - Required Skills (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- Excellent communication and leadership skills, with the ability to inspire at every level of the organisation.
- Strong strategic and analytical thinking — you see the whole business, not just the product.
- Deep understanding of product development, live operations, growth metrics and market dynamics.
- A growth mindset: proactive, adaptable, and focused on long-term success.
- Ability to make and own tough decisions — and to bring people with you.
- Passion for games, entertainment, and creating experiences that delight millions.
- Fluency in English (written & spoken).

metadata.name: Publish - Required Experience (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- Proven track record in a C-level or senior product executive role (e.g., CPO, VP of Product) — in the gaming or digital entertainment industry.
- Proven gaming industry experience: understanding of hybrid casual and casual games.
- Success leading teams through critical phases, reversing negative trends, and/or driving growth or product recovery.

metadata.name: Pubish - What Do We Offer
metadata.value:
- Employment relationship with Outfit7 Limited for an indefinite period of time, with a six-month probationary period.
- Diverse work with exciting daily challenges, working as part of a team in a fun, relaxed environment.
- Participation in attractive projects.
- Development opportunities through training, seminars, and conferences.
- Choice of sport, fitness, educational, and entertainment activities outside of work.
- Healthy meals in the office.
- Flexible working hours.
- 24 days of paid vacation.
- Participation in a discretionary bonus plan.
- Christmas bonus (subject to internal company policies).

metadata.value: Got a passion for data science and mathematical modeling? Blessed with an excellent eye for details and for opportunities hiding in data? Want to add your talents for ideation and implementation to an already stellar team? Then you might be just the exceptional data scientist we’re searching for!

metadata.value:
- Create mathematical models for better understanding the value of Outfit7’s users and of the profitability of Outfit7’s marketing campaigns.
- Optimize data processing workflows to improve efficiency and performance.
- Drive operational improvements within analytical processes and methodology, as well as campaign improvements.
- Help set priorities within the team based on business impact.
- Work efficiently as part of the team.

metadata.value:
- Talent for data science methodology and practice, as well as storytelling capability.
- Proficiency in reporting and explaining complex results to people from different professional backgrounds.
- Excellent attention to detail and ability to find opportunities for improvements in dashboards and to follow through on changes.
- Positive, make-it-happen, lead-by-example attitude.
- Good communication and presentation skills, with fluency in the English language (both written and spoken).

metadata.name: Publish - Required Experience (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- 3+ years of experience working in data science using statistical methods.
- Experience with ML for modeling more complex concepts.
- Experience in data instrumentation and data collection, as well as in the use of tools for analyzing data (e.g. R, Python).
- Experience in marketing data science (or similar role) would be a plus.

metadata.name: Job category (area)
metadata.value: Marketing & Publishing

metadata.name: Recuiter to display
metadata.value: Neja Rozman

metadata.name: Division
metadata.value: PlayValley

metadata.name: Employment Type
metadata.value: Full-time

metadata.name: Publish - Description (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
The mission of a new IP division within Outfit7, called PlayValley, is creating new IPs through rapid prototyping and bold experimentation.
We are looking for a Growth Product Manager with a passion for gaming and a deep curiosity for data. In this role, you will connect the dots between user acquisition funnels and in-game metrics, turning raw data from casual game prototypes into actionable insights. You’ll evaluate  whether a game has real potential, explain why, and guide changes to UA campaigns, audiences, channels, and even in-game features to enhance growth.
You will be the bridge between creative prototypes and UA execution, ensuring high-quality traffic reaches games that engage and retain players. This is a role for someone motivated, analytical, and excited to shape the growth of a casual gaming portfolio.

metadata.name: Publish - Responsibilities (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- Provide the technical instructions for theUA team. You’ll be the one to spot when a campaign is wasting money on low-quality placements and propose the most viable course of action.
- Handle the data side of early market validation tests. You'll set up the tracking, monitor the results, and report back on whether a casual game prototype has the prospect to scale.
- Identify Funnel Leaks - in case of a gap between the games, countries, audiences, you are the one who digs into the cohorts. You’ll find out if it's a technical bug, a targeting mismatch, or a game issue.
- Be the link between ads and in-game metrics. You’ll build the reporting that proves which specific ad creatives are bringing in players who actually play, which indicators show potential, and why cohorts perform differently.
- Push the UA team to move beyond "cheap clicks" and vanity CPIs. You’ll guide them toward building a profitable product and find players who actually fit the casual game mechanics at hand.
- Collaborate with Distribution and Product teams to identify opportunities to improve distribution.
- Develop robust experimentation frameworks and perform analyses to support profit growth and distribution optimization.
- Advise the Marketing team on test design and experimental setups based on data insights.
- Analyze results from various experiments and tests.

metadata.name: Publish - Required Skills (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- Being able to make decisions based on data and measurable results rather than intuition or opinion.
- Having the ability to optimize growth funnels and acquisition strategies for profitability, focusing on finding high-quality, engaged players.
- Understanding advertising algorithms deeply and knowing which levers to pull to improve campaign performance and efficiency.
- Demonstrating analytical objectivity and the confidence to recommend ending a project or prototype that doesn’t meet market validation benchmarks.
- Possessing expertise in casual gaming and knowing performance benchmarks for casual and puzzle titles.
- Being able to distinguish between high-volume hyper-casual traffic and high-retention casual audiences and act based on these insights.
- Communicating complex findings clearly and effectively to various audiences, including company leadership, using actionable insights.
- Structuring complex analyses into coherent frameworks and translating large datasets into practical, data-driven recommendations.

metadata.name: Publish - Required Experience (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- 3+ years of hands-on experience in mobile gaming analytics or growth, with advanced skills in SQL and querying BigQuery independently.
- Highly proficient with analytics and visualization tools such as Firebase, Tableau, Looker Studio, and advertising dashboards like Meta and AppLovin.
- Possessing deep, practical experience with MMPs, particularly Singular, and knowing how to map users to in-game milestones.
- Having a proven track record in running the data side of soft launches or market validation tests for casual games and helping titles scale from prototype to global launch.
- Being familiar with UA execution, including extensive experience in Meta Ads Manager and other advertising tools, and understanding the trade-offs between broad targeting, ROAS optimization, and targeted campaigns.
- Having strong auction and market intelligence, including knowledge of CPM and CPI variations across regions and genres, and the ability to adjust testing budgets to determine true retention floors in competitive markets.

metadata.name: Pubish - What Do We Offer
metadata.value:
- Employment relationship with a legal entity within Outfit7 group for an indefinite period of time, with a six-month probationary period.
- Diverse work with exciting daily challenges, working as part of a team in a fun, relaxed environment.
- Participation in attractive projects.
- Development opportunities through training, seminars, and conferences
- Choice of sport, fitness, educational, and entertainment activities outside of work.
- Healthy meals in the office
- Flexible working hours
- 24 days of paid vacation
- Participation in a discretionary bonus plan.
- Christmas bonus (subject to internal policies).
- We are open to exploring remote work possibilities, subject to the specifics of the candidate's location and preferences.

metadata.name: Publish - Description (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
Love when everything just works? So do we. That's why we're looking for an IT Business Systems Analyst who's a natural problem solver, a strong collaborator, and someone who thrives on making things run smoothly behind the scenes.
Your main focus will be keeping business applications reliable, spotting issues early, and turning feedback into meaningful improvements. You'll play an important role in helping teams get the most out of their tools — bringing clarity to complexity and making sure everything keeps running smoothly.

metadata.name: Publish - Responsibilities (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- Provide support to users across business applications (ERP, HR, and other systems) by troubleshooting issues, resolving incidents and requests, and helping users understand system processes and functionality.
- Collaborate with teams across the company to understand needs and translate them into data solutions.
- Coordinate effectively with internal teams and external partners during issue resolution, ensuring clear, structured, and timely communication throughout the process.
- Manage user access and permissions across business applications.
- Configure and optimize business applications based on business needs and improvement opportunities.
- Prepare and maintain dashboards and reports (SQL, SOQL, Power BI) using data from business applications.
- Participate in implementation projects, define requirements, and ensure a data-driven approach when introducing new features.
- Test system changes and new functionalities, ensuring data accuracy and consistency across environments.
- Maintain and improve data integrations to ensure reliable data flow between systems.
- Keep documentation and data logic clear, structured, and up to date.

metadata.name: Publish - Required Skills (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- Problem-solving mindset with confidence in working with data and troubleshooting issues.
- Ability to work independently, prioritize tasks, and take ownership of work.
- A proactive, hands-on approach and motivation to build and improve processes.
- A good understanding of business processes and how they connect across departments.
- Strong communication skills to work with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
- Fluency in English, both written and spoken.

metadata.name: Publish - Required Experience (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- 2+ years of experience with business applications and user support.
- Experience with hands-on software troubleshooting.
- Experience with IT project management. 
- Experience in working with ERP, CRM (Salesforce), SQL SMS Studio, and/or Power BI is a plus.

metadata.name: Pubish - What Do We Offer
metadata.value:
- Employment relationship with Ekipa2, d.o.o. (an Outfit7 subsidiary) for an indefinite period of time, with a six-month probationary period.
- Diverse work with exciting daily challenges, working as part of a team in a fun, relaxed environment.
- Participation in attractive projects.
- Development opportunities through training, seminars, and conferences.
- Choice of sport, fitness, educational, and entertainment activities outside of work.
- Healthy meals in the office.
- Flexible working hours.
- 24 days of paid vacation.
- Participation in a discretionary bonus plan.
- Christmas bonus (subject to internal policies).

metadata.name: Publish - Description (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
Love when everything just works? So do we. That's why we're looking for an IT Business Systems Analyst who's a natural problem solver, a strong collaborator, and someone who thrives on making things run smoothly behind the scenes.
Your main focus will be building dashboards and reports based on data from our business applications. You'll ensure that data across systems is structured, reliable, and useful for reporting and decision-making. You'll support daily operations, improve existing solutions, and contribute to new implementations. 
The long-term goal — to become the go-to person for data and reporting within the team.

metadata.name: Publish - Responsibilities (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- Be responsible for data and reporting across business applications (ERP, HR, and other systems).
- Manage and develop SQL views in the ERP system, supporting financial reporting and improving underlying data structures.
- Work with SOQL queries to extract and analyze data from Salesforce.
- Maintain and expand the Data Lakehouse, including integrations from ERP and other systems.
- Build and maintain Power BI dashboards, combining data from multiple sources into clear insights.
- Structure, clean, and integrate historical and legacy data into centralized models.
- Collaborate with teams across the company to understand needs and translate them into data solutions.
- Support implementation projects, define requirements, and ensure a data-driven approach.
- Test system changes and new functionalities to ensure data accuracy and consistency.
- Maintain and improve data integrations to ensure reliable data flow.
- Keep documentation and data logic clear, structured, and up to date.

metadata.name: Publish - Required Skills (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- Problem-solving mindset with confidence in working with data and troubleshooting issues.
- Ability to work independently, prioritize tasks, and take ownership of work.
- A proactive, hands-on approach and motivation to build and improve processes.
- Curiosity and willingness to learn, especially in understanding business applications and how data connects across systems.
- Strong communication skills to work with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
- Fluency in English, both written and spoken.

metadata.name: Publish - Required Experience (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- 2+ years of experience with business applications, SQL databases (e.g., SQL Server / SSMS) and Power BI (or similar BI tools), including writing queries and building dashboards.
- Solid understanding of data modelling and how data is structured for reporting.
- Experience working with data pipelines and centralized data environments (e.g., Data Warehouse or Data Lakehouse).
- Experience in working with ERP, CRM (Salesforce), or similar business applications is a plus.

metadata.name: Publish - Description (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
We are looking for a seasoned and skilled Senior Business Development Expert to join our international mobile game development team. As a member of the Business Development department, you will be an expert in establishing, managing, and upgrading our relationships with app stores, publishing platforms, and key industry partners.
In this role, you will use your broad knowledge to influence direction and create innovative solutions that contribute to achieving company goals. We are looking for a professional who can recognize company-wide opportunities and impact our most important projects. We value a solution-oriented mindset and the ability to navigate complex business relations in a results-oriented environment.

metadata.name: Publish - Responsibilities (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- Manage and upgrade company relationships with app stores and publishing platforms to optimize business performance.
- Initiate and manage demanding cross-departmental projects from ideation through execution.
- Recognize company-wide opportunities and provide an analytical perspective to help prioritize work based on business impact.
- Propose the most optimal solutions for challenging situations while keeping business needs and company goals in mind.
- Partner with different business segments, including production, distribution, and sales, to ensure alignment.
- Maintain familiarity with regulatory and legal concerns by working closely with the company’s Legal department.
- Find innovative ways to perform work and propose improvements to tools and workflows as needed.

metadata.name: Publish - Required Skills (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- Being able to understand the “big picture” of the most challenging projects and how they connect with department and company goals.

metadata.name: Publish - Description (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
A gamer yourself, you know what makes a great game. And as a Game Designer, you’d never compromise on that.
You understand what motivates and excites players, and what works well in gameplay – and what doesn’t. What sets you apart from the rest is that you know what and how can be improved. Confident in your skills and collaborative in your approach, you can take an idea and run with it, or work with the team to brainstorm and create something truly great together.
Sound like you? Then apply now and let’s work on awesome games together.

metadata.name: Publish - Responsibilities (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- Manage the end-to-end game design process for Talking Tom and Friends IP games.
- Collaborate with product, art, and engineering leads to implement gameplay mechanics, monetization systems, and player progression.
- Implement creative vision and maintain design quality across multiple projects simultaneously.
- Analyze gameplay data, player feedback, and market trends to provide information for design decisions.
- Champion fun, intuitive, accessible gameplay tailored to mobile platforms.
- Present clear documentation and pitches to internal stakeholders and team members.

metadata.value:

- Ability to thrive in a fast-moving environment.
- Passion for mobile games—especially casual and hybrid casual genres.
- Deep understanding of game design principles, systems thinking, and behavioral economics.
- Strong understanding of player behaviors and motivations across target audiences.
- Strong balance between creative intuition and analytical thinking.
- Comfortable taking ownership and pushing projects forward with minimal oversight.
- Excellent communication and collaboration skills and ability to navigate cross-disciplinary areas.
- Positive, make-it-happen, lead-by-example attitude.

metadata.name: Publish - Required Experience (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- 5+ years of professional experience in game design.
- Proven track record shipping mobile titles, ideally in the casual, and/or hybrid casual space.
- Hands-on experience with Unity or other game engines.
- Familiarity with live ops, A/B testing, and working with game analytics.
- Bonus: Background in UX, product design, and/or economy design.

metadata.name: Division
metadata.value: Marketing

metadata.name: Employment Type
metadata.value: Full-time

metadata.name: Publish - Description (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
The Team
The Growth team is focused on maximising the potential of all titles Outfit7 ships. It brings together user acquisition, creative strategy, ASO, data, and product collaboration under one roof, with full-funnel accountability for the games in its portfolio. We operate across all lifecycle stages, from R&D and prototype validation through to global scale, across a range of mobile game formats.

You are the senior growth authority in the room. You don’t just run marketing growth function, you define how it operates and where it goes next. You understand that paid and organic UA are one system, that measurement is a strategic asset, and that the difference between a good growth leader and a great one is the ability to anticipate problems before they surface in the data. You’ve built teams, shaped department-level plans and goals, and earned the trust of leadership and platforms alike. 
As Director of Growth Marketing, you will set the direction of player acquisition and organic growth function across all active titles and the R&D pipeline. You manage the team, set the team goals, and serve as the primary growth voice in the department. 
If you want to shape the growth of games played by hundreds of millions of players, get in touch.

metadata.name: Publish - Responsibilities (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- Lead the Growth Marketing team, setting direction across paid and organic channels, translating company goals into team-level plans, and aligning well in advance with the VP, peers, and team leads.
- Set the KPI framework and targets for the Paid Growth and Organic Growth leads, hold them accountable, and provide the information, context, and direction they need to operate effectively.
- Allocate the budget across paid UA, ASO, and influencer programmes, ensuring spend is structured to maximise LTV efficiency across the portfolio.
- Set the direction for paid growth: channel mix, bid frameworks, budget management, network diversification, and new channel evaluation.
- Set the direction for organic growth: ASO strategy, featuring opportunities, platform partnership programmes, and LiveOps promotional content alignment.
- Contribute to the direction for platform opportunities with Apple and Google at Apple and Google, providing the growth expertise needed to secure featuring opportunities, premium tools access, and partnership programmes, and aligning internal goals with external platform priorities.
- Partner with the Analytics Lead on LTV forecasting and growth funnel reporting, ensuring the team operates on a shared, reliable measurement framework.
- Operate as the primary counterpart to the Director of Marketing: own the creative-to-UA briefing workflow and ensure campaign and creative pipelines are aligned.
- Ensure the Growth team is structured and staffed appropriately, has the right mix of skills and knowledge, and has access to the learning and development they need.
- Actively develop the leads and senior specialists within the team, understanding their strengths and longer-term career plans, recognising performance, and identifying and addressing performance issues constructively.
- Anticipate growth risks and opportunities before they appear in the data, propose solutions, and represent the team in internal communication channels.
- Embed company culture within the department, enable effective collaboration across teams, encourage diverse perspectives, and positively influence how the Growth team is perceived internally and externally.

metadata.name: Publish - Required Skills (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- A systems thinker who sees paid and organic as one growth engine and structures the team, the budget, and the priorities accordingly.
- The strategic depth and analytical confidence to set direction independently, defend it with data, and adjust it when the evidence calls for it.
- Proven people leadership: you develop leads, not just manage them, and you think about succession, team structure, and capability building as part of the role.
- Deep expertise in mobile measurement, including predictive LTV, incrementality, privacy-first attribution, and the trade-offs that come with each.
- The communication and credibility to represent the Growth team in internal communication channels, translate company strategy into team goals, and challenge direction constructively when needed.
- A cross-functional operating style and the ability to build trust across creative, product, analytics, and platform partners without relying on formal authority.

metadata.name: Publish - Required Experience (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- 7+ years of experience in mobile growth or user acquisition, with at least 4 years in a senior leadership role with direct accountability for both paid and organic channels.
- Demonstrated success in setting and delivering on portfolio-level growth targets across multiple titles and lifecycle stages.
- Experience managing senior specialists or leads, including setting goals, developing talent, and overseeing team structure and staffing.
- Deep understanding of mobile measurement in a privacy-constrained environment: MMP configuration, SKAdNetwork, iOS ATT, and LTV modelling.
- Experience managing across paid and organic functions simultaneously, with the expertise to lead both through direction and provide credible guidance in the most critical situations.

metadata.name: Pubish - What Do We Offer
metadata.value:
- Employment relationship with Ekipa2 d.o.o. for an indefinite period of time, with a six-month probation period.
- Diverse work with exciting daily challenges, as part of a team in a fun, relaxed environment.
- Participation in attractive projects with real ownership and cross-functional impact.
- Development opportunities through training, seminars, and conferences.
- Choice of sport, fitness, educational, and entertainment activities outside of work.
- Healthy meals in the office.
- Flexible working hours.
- 24 days of paid vacation.
- Participation in a discretionary bonus plan.
- Christmas bonus (subject to internal company policies).

metadata.name: Employment Type

metadata.name: Publish - Description (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
The Team
The Growth team is focused on maximising the potential of all titles Outfit7 ships. It brings together user acquisition, creative strategy, ASO, data, and product collaboration under one roof, with full-funnel accountability for the games in its portfolio. We operate across all lifecycle stages, from R&D and prototype validation through to global scale, across a range of mobile game formats.

You think in funnels, not campaigns. Growth rate is your north star, and you know that installs are just one input into the metric that actually matters. From paid UA to product-led loops, you’re comfortable pulling any lever in the stack, and you know which one to pull when. You bring structure to ambiguity, data to gut feeling, and a clear point of view to every cross-functional room you’re in. 
As a Senior Growth Marketing Manager, you will scale a game or a portfolio of new titles, acting as a strategic counterpart to the product owner. You will leverage paid performance marketing and non-paid growth levers to drive audience growth and push performance further.
If you’re ready to develop the full acquisition-to-retention journey for games played by millions, get in touch.

metadata.name: Publish - Responsibilities (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- Help with the full-funnel growth strategy for assigned titles, setting quarterly plans aligned with the game roadmap and LiveOps calendar, with growth rate (DAU/MAU/revenue) as your north star.
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including game leads, product managers, and creative teams, to define growth objectives, KPI targets, and delivery timelines.
- Implement the end-to-end strategy across all acquisition channels: paid UA across key networks, organic search and browse, brand partnerships, influencer marketing, and LiveOps promotion.
- Drive creative testing: briefing hypotheses, iterating on results, identifying fatigue patterns early, and building scalable creative production pipelines with the creative team.
- Manage the ASO strategy for assigned titles beyond metadata updates, including store A/B testing, CPP management, platform relationships, and featuring opportunities.
- Design attribution strategy in a privacy-first environment, including MMP configuration, SKAdNetwork setup, and iOS ATT constraints.
- Build and interpret cohort analyses and LTV models to diagnose growth bottlenecks and guide prioritisation across acquisition and retention levers.
- Partner with the production product owner to align UA spend and creative angles with the LiveOps calendar, and collaborate on retention-driving product events and re-engagement campaigns.
- Support the R&D pipeline through prototype testing, contributing targeting strategy, channel-fit assessment, and early retention signal analysis.
- Build team playbooks, identify new channels to test at scale, and mentor junior team members on how UA decisions connect to full-funnel outcomes.

metadata.name: Publish - Required Skills (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- A full-funnel mindset: you understand that acquisition metrics are inputs to growth rate, not the goal itself, and you make decisions accordingly.
- Strong analytical ability with the confidence to build your own models, and turn complex data into clear recommendations.
- Deep comfort with mobile measurement in a privacy-constrained environment, including predictive LTV, incrementality thinking, and the limits of last-click attribution.
- A structured approach to experimentation: clear hypotheses, honest read-outs, and a bias toward learning over confirmation.
- The ability to influence product, data, and creative teams without formal authority, operating as a genuine partner rather than a requester.
- Strong communication skills, written and spoken, with the ability to translate performance data into strategic narratives for senior stakeholders.

metadata.name: Publish - Required Experience (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- 5+ years of hands-on experience in growth marketing or user acquisition for mobile games or apps, ideally IAP or subscription-based titles.
- Proven track record managing titles across multiple lifecycle stages, from prototype and soft launch through to global scale.
- Demonstrated success in optimising campaigns toward ROAS and LTV targets across multiple major networks simultaneously.
- Experience working with MMPs and navigating iOS ATT, SKAdNetwork, and privacy-first measurement constraints.
- Hands-on experience with ASO, store A/B testing, and organic growth levers beyond keyword optimisation is a strong advantage.
- Experience mentoring or leading other UA or growth team members is a plus.

metadata.name: Pubish - What Do We Offer

- Employment relationship with Ekipa2 d.o.o. for an indefinite period of time, with a six-month probation period.
- Diverse work with exciting daily challenges, as part of a team in a fun, relaxed environment.
- Participation in attractive projects with real ownership and cross-functional impact.
- Development opportunities through training, seminars, and conferences.
- Choice of sport, fitness, educational, and entertainment activities outside of work.

metadata.name: Publish - Description (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
Where data meets decisions!
Join us as a Senior Marketing Data Scientist and turn complex data into smarter marketing moves. From experimentation frameworks to profitability models, you’ll contribute to success and help shape data-driven marketing at Outfit7.

metadata.name: Publish - Responsibilities (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- Collaborate with Distribution and Product teams to identify opportunities to improve distribution.
- Proactively discover and drive new data-driven approaches to optimize distribution.
- Work on ad hoc projects that support company strategy.
- Present and share new methodologies and best practices internally and externally.
- Build and maintain clean, scalable data pipelines and internal tools that reduce manual work, minimize errors, and enable at-scale optimization.
- Develop robust experimentation frameworks and perform analyses to support profit growth and distribution optimization.
- Communicate insights clearly, challenge outdated practices, and lead pragmatic improvements.
- Optimize data processing workflows to improve efficiency and performance.
- Advise the Marketing team on test design and experimental setups based on data insights.
- Contribute analytical perspectives to discussions and suggest improvements to campaign performance.
- Analyze results from various experiments and tests.
- Implement mathematical models to optimize the performance of Outfit7's marketing campaigns.

metadata.name: Publish - Required Skills (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to understand data and identify key trends.
- Advanced data analysis skills, particularly in SQL and R or Python.
- Ability to work efficiently in a fast-changing environment.
- Excellent communication and presentation skills; fluent in English (written and spoken).
- Proven ability to structure complex problems, conduct thorough analyses, and generate actionable insights from large datasets.
- Proficiency in reporting and explaining complex results to stakeholders from diverse professional backgrounds.
- Excellent attention to detail and a continuous-improvement mindset.
- A positive, proactive, lead-by-example attitude.

metadata.name: Publish - Required Experience (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- 5+ years of experience in a data scientist role, ideally in mobile gaming, performance marketing or related fields (you should know how to use data to help with business decisions). 
- Proficiency in R or Python, primarily for statistical analysis, data manipulation, and visualisation.
- Experience with data analysis, reporting, and monitoring.
- Experience with data-oriented tools, such as SQL; familiarity with DBT is a plus.
- Proven ability to structure complex problems, conduct thorough analyses, and generate actionable insights from large datasets.
- Experience working with teams from different departments.
- Experience in leading a smaller team would be a plus.

- Development opportunities through training, seminars, and conferences.

metadata.value: We're on the hunt for a Senior Product Manager with deep knowledge of the mobile gaming landscape to lead the implementation of compelling game titles. If you're passionate about game experiences that resonate with players, blending business with creativity, and driving a product's journey from concept to launch, we want you!

metadata.name: Publish - Responsibilities (Recruiter)
metadata.value:

- Drive and guide the team regarding which ideas to pursue, using expertise, clear reasoning, and a deep understanding of the market and of the business goals.
- Ensure the delivery of agreed-upon output by setting priorities, maintaining focus, and asking the right product questions.
- Understand the vision of the game, and communicate it clearly to the team and to stakeholders.
- Provide the team with suggestions and solutions for improving ideas and game features.
- Maintain and prioritise the ideas and features backlog.
- Identify and implement ways to improve working practices in the department in order to increase efficiency, focus on the right priorities, and maintain a positive and collaborative atmosphere.

- Ability to independently troubleshoot, prioritise, and address challenges that arise during the product lifecycle.
- Proficiency with tools like JIRA and Trello, or other project management software.
- Excellent ability to balance business, creative needs, timelines, and user experience in the creation of new games.
- Strong communication skills, with fluency in the English language (both written and spoken).
- First-rate interpersonal skills, with the ability to communicate effectively with colleagues and partners.
- Ability to focus on the bigger picture  while simultaneously paying close attention to details.
- Ability to prepare presentations, product specifications, and work on product requirements.
- Passion for mobile games and an excellent understanding of player needs.

- Experience in cross-departmental collaboration.

metadata.name: Publish - Description (Recruiter)
metadata.value: Full-funnel UA is your playground, and you know every corner — from big-picture vision to hands-on execution. You live for the challenge of managing and optimizing campaigns, squeezing every ounce of performance from every channel. Data isn’t just numbers to you — it’s the map to your next breakthrough, guiding cross-functional collaboration and sparking ideas that scale. Ready to transform traffic into traction and planning into unstoppable growth? Then get in touch!

metadata.name: Publish - Responsibilities (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- Execute user acquisition (UA) strategies for games at all stages of development, including prototyping, soft launch, and global release — with a focus on titles driven by in-app purchases (IAP) or subscriptions.
- Manage the UA strategy for each product based on its specific goals and lifecycle stage.
- Plan, launch, and optimize UA campaigns to meet key performance indicators (KPIs) and drive scalable growth.
- Collaborate closely with product, creative, and analytics teams to design and execute tests that enhance both user acquisition effectiveness and overall product performance.
- Analyze campaign performance data to uncover insights and continuously improve both UA efficiency and return on investment.
- Stay informed on industry trends, emerging platforms, and market shifts in order to proactively identify opportunities and mitigate potential risks.
- Share knowledge, insights, and best practices across teams to support personal and collective growth in UA capabilities.
- Candidate with experience as a lead would be considered in a hybrid role, also  leading a small team of User Acquisition experts.

metadata.name: Publish - Required Skills (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- Deep understanding of user acquisition strategies and best practices within the mobile app ecosystem.
- Strong passion for performance marketing, with a focus on data-driven growth.
- Analytical mindset with the ability to extract actionable insights, identify trends, and detect anomalies in complex datasets.
- Solid grasp of predictive models such as Lifetime Value (LTV) and Return on Ad Spend (ROAS).
- Skilled at setting clear priorities based on potential impact and business value.
- Strong communication skills, with fluency in English, both written and spoken.
- Nice to have: Ability to manage not only your own responsibilities, but also those of a broader team.

metadata.name: Publish - Required Experience (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- 5+ years of hands-on experience in user acquisition for in-app purchase (IAP) or subscription-based mobile games or apps.
- Proven track record of managing products through multiple lifecycle stages — from prototype and soft launch to global launch — is a strong advantage.
- Demonstrated success in optimizing campaigns toward Return on Ad Spend (ROAS).
- Experience in managing and scaling campaigns across a variety of channels, including Meta, Google Ads, TikTok, - Apple Search Ads (ASA), and ad networks, and in working with mobile measurement partners (MMPs).
- Familiarity with privacy regulations and data limitations, including experience optimizing campaigns without access to user-level data on post-install events/ROAS.
- Experience in leading a team of other UA experts is a plus, but not a requirement.

metadata.name: Pubish - What Do We Offer
metadata.value:
- Employment relationship with Ekipa2 d.o.o. for an indefinite period of time, with a six-month probationary period.
- Diverse work with exciting daily challenges, working as part of a team in a fun, relaxed environment.
- Participation in attractive projects.
- Development opportunities through training, seminars, and conferences.
- Choice of sport, fitness, educational, and entertainment activities outside of work.
- Healthy meals in the office.
- Flexible working hours.
- 24 days of paid vacation.
- Participation in a discretionary bonus plan.
- Christmas bonus (subject to internal company policies).

metadata.name: Publish - Description (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
Are you organized, practical, and eager to learn new things? We’re looking for a new Workplace Experience Coordinator!

The role requires the ability to juggle a wide range of priorities and tasks, and keep everything organized along the way. Great people skills and a desire to work as part of a team in a fast-paced environment are also a must. So if you’re adaptable, energetic, and excited about the idea of a job in which no two days are the same, then read on. This might be the one for you!

metadata.name: Publish - Responsibilities (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- Provide information and assistance at the reception desk (entry cards, contacting employees, etc.) and act as the main contact person for resolving office-related issues.
- Provide administrative support (the classification and processing of documents, purchasing office supplies and other materials, document scanning and photocopying, etc.).
- Care for the cleanliness, tidiness, and orderliness of the business premises.
- Manage the new employee process in cooperation with other teams (e.g. HR, IT, marketing).
- Gather products and prepare packages for employee appreciation programs and business partner gifts.
- Support office managers with tasks related to employee travel and education.
- Help with deliveries and shipping, including tracking the process via our digital system.

metadata.name: Publish - Required Skills (Recruiter)
metadata.value:
- Excellent understanding of other people's behavior and needs.
- Strong communication skills, with fluency in the English language, both written and spoken.
- Ability to work effectively as part of a team.
- Ability to work in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.
- Passion for the mobile games and entertainment industries is a plus.

metadata.name: Publish - Required Experience (Recruiter)
metadata.value: - No prior experience required.

metadata.name: Pubish - What Do We Offer
metadata.value:
- Student cooperation agreement with Ekipa2 d.o.o. (an Outfit7 subsidiary)
- Diverse work with exciting challenges, working in a fun, relaxed environment.
- The opportunity to obtain valuable experience through temporary and occasional work.
- Participation in attractive projects.
